summary: The following modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers target three core processes in the case: (a) reducing rapid irritability to minor provocations, (b) shrinking trigger-reactivity, (c) improving post-episode recovery and repair, (d) easing self-directed distress, (e) closing the gap between intention and behavior in interpersonal exchanges, (f) providing healthier immediate coping after conflicts, and (g) enhancing sleep-based affect regulation. Each lever is a practical, short-term behavior or ecological change with measurable indicators and clear links to the stated variables (C01–C07).

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a daily 10–15 minute emotion-regulation practice (diaphragmatic breathing, urge surfing, cognitive reappraisal) plus 2 in-the-moment prompts (pause and reframe) during triggering moments (e.g., dishes, tone).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthens top-down regulatory control to dampen amygdala-driven irritability, improves recovery after episodes, and reduces harsh self-evaluation by promoting adaptive appraisal.
solutions.measurement: Daily irritability rating (0–10); number of reactive episodes; post-episode distress rating; brief ecological momentary prompts compliance.
solutions.safety_notes: No acute safety concerns; monitor distress; escalate if self-harm risk emerges.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01, C03, C04; implement daily emotion regulation practice and in-the-moment prompts.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish a 60-second pause before responding when triggered by specific cues (household tasks, partner tone), combined with a simple scripted calm-response (I-statements after a brief pause). Maintain a trigger log to identify recurring cues.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Modifies stimulus-response dynamics to reduce automatic reactivity and relational strain, enabling more deliberate communication.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of pause usage per trigger; self-reported trigger magnitude; partner-rated reactivity.
solutions.safety_notes: None specific; ensure space for safety if escalation occurs.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01, C02, C07; implement pause-before-response and trigger logging.

solutions.solution_candidate: After conflict, execute a 5-minute cooling-off followed by a brief repair conversation using I-statements and validation (“I felt X when Y happened; I want Z”).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured repair enhances emotional recovery and aligns behavior with stated values, reducing negative relational impact.
solutions.measurement: Number of repair conversations; partner-reported warmth; post-episode distress.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for any escalation in conflict dynamics.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C03, C05, C07; implement post-conflict repair script.

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily 5-minute self-compassion journaling and supportive self-talk to address self-directed distress and alienation from self.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Self-compassion reduces harsh self-criticism and distress, supporting adaptive regulation of self-perception after episodes.
solutions.measurement: Self-criticism score (brief scale) and distress rating; adherence to journaling.
solutions.safety_notes: None specific; monitor for any worsening mood; escalate if self-harm risk emerges.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C04; implement daily self-compassion journaling.

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and practice I-statements, reflective listening, and repair attempts; implement a 10-minute daily couple check-in emphasizing warmth and needs.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances alignment between intended kindness and enacted behavior; reduces relational strain through skilled communication.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of I-statements; partner ratings of warmth; number of repair attempts.
solutions.safety_notes: If conflict becomes unsafe, seek professional guidance.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C05, C07; implement couple communication skills training and practice.

solutions.solution_candidate: Replace secret crying with a structured time-out (5 minutes) followed by a guided re-engagement plan (calm breathing, and then a short check-in with partner using neutral language).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Provides space for regulation while preserving opportunity for later repair; reduces hidden withdrawal and immediate post-conflict distress.
solutions.measurement: Frequency and duration of private crying episodes; time-to-re-engage; time-out adherence.
solutions.safety_notes: If distress escalates or self-harm risk emerges, seek urgent help.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C03, C06; implement structured time-out and re-engagement plan.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep regularization: consistent bed/wake times, reduce evening screen exposure, and caffeine cutoff after 2 PM to improve emotional reactivity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Sleep optimization enhances prefrontal control over emotional responses, reducing baseline irritability and cue-reactivity.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/quality (diary or wearable); morning irritability rating.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01, C02; implement sleep hygiene routine with monitoring.
